name: KUU BUNSHIN NO HAETORI (VOID REPLICATION DEATH TRAP)

description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu

Rank: 13

Chakra Cost: 14

Learn DC: 29 | Successes: 7

Perform: 17 | Perform DC: 33

Components: C, H

This forbidden technique was once created for the purpose of abduction and/or assassination. The clone created resembles a physical creature, but underneath its surface lies a small pocket dimension. Unless otherwise noted, it is identical to a Kage Bunshin.

Appearance: Creatures who can See Through Chakra see the void clone as a shell filled with emptiness, as if nothing existed inside the clone.

Control: A void clone can move no more than 100 ft. from their creator.

Statistics: Void clones have a poor flight speed equal to your base land speed, and can walk on water. These benefits are lost while holding a creature (see below).

Combat: In addition to a clone's normal attacks and techniques, the clone can attempt to suck in an adjacent creature no larger than itself as a standard action (Fortitude negates, CR applies). If successful, the creature is trapped inside the clone's pocket dimension.

A void clone can only hold one creature at a time.

While Holding a Creature: The void clone gains bonus HP equal to the captured creature's HD while its being held, but cannot walk on water or fly while holding a creature.

A trapped creature can see what happens 'outside', but cannot communicate with the outside world, and it cannot harm the void clone from the inside.

If the clone takes damage, the creature trapped inside suffers double that damage, and the damage cannot be reduced or mitigated in any way. Force damage dealt to the clone is not transferred to the trapped creature.

As a free action while holding a creature, the clone can blow itself up, dealing 3d6 force damage in a 30ft. radius around it (Reflex halves, CR applies). The creature trapped inside the clone takes double damage, and cannot make a save to escape the blast.

Defeat and Dismissal: When a void clone is ended (its duration ends, is reduced to 0 hp, etc) it disappears in a puff of smoke. Any creature still trapped inside is freed, reappearing on the square the clone was in with no further ill effects.
